steps.title: Recognize the equation structure
steps.content: The equation $13x + 14y = 423$ follows the form $(\text{price}_1) \cdot x + (\text{price}_2) \cdot y = \text{total}$. This is the standard form for situations involving two types of items sold at different unit prices, where the total represents the combined revenue.

steps.title: Identify what the variables represent
steps.content: Since 13 and 14 are reasonable prices in dollars for shirts, they must be the unit prices. The variables $x$ and $y$ are therefore quantities—the number of each type of shirt sold—and 423 is the total revenue. This immediately eliminates choices C and D, which incorrectly claim that $y$ is a price rather than a quantity.

steps.title: Determine which shirt type corresponds to y
steps.content: The coefficient paired with $y$ is 14. In the standard form, this coefficient represents the unit price of whatever $y$ counts. Since 14 dollars per shirt is the higher price, and long-sleeved shirts are typically more expensive than short-sleeved shirts, the variable $y$ represents the number of long-sleeved shirts sold. That matches choice A — A is correct.
